attacking_style: Conservative counterattacker who waits for opponents to crack before moving, typically in final 1-6km of climbs. Lacks sustained attacking power but capitalizes on pacing discipline to regain dropped positions.
team_tactics: Relies heavily on Sky/INEOS tempo control and domestique support; when isolated or without full train, becomes vulnerable to harassment attacks. Team commits resources to bonus second sprints and selective leadout work.
terrain_preference: Excels in time trials maintaining form into late 30s, performs best on sustained climbs where pacing discipline matters. Exposed on explosive gradients (24%+), technical descents, gravel sections, and variable-effort terrain.
vulnerability: Chronic crash susceptibility in technical/wet conditions (6+ crashes documented). Loses contact on short explosive pitches and variable terrain. Fades in final week mountain stages, particularly steep gradients in sustained TTs.
key_patterns: Repeatedly dropped on climbs but regains contact through superior pacing, documented across multiple Tours (5+ instances)
Crashes in technical conditions spanning 2017-2023, including wet descents, gravel, early-race positioning errors
Strongest in time trials even at 36+ years, multiple near-wins and overall victories via ITT
Requires team support when isolated; struggles without INEOS train against harassment tactics (2022-2023 Vuelta)
Transitions to domestique role mid-race when GC compromised, showing tactical flexibility (Giro 2023, TdF 2019)
Exploits final-kilometer opportunities opportunistically rather than launching sustained attacks from distance
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 Vuelta showed multiple crashes, handling errors, dropped on climbs by harassment tactics, and poor demeanor. Lost climbing form evident compared to competitive 2022 Tour podium and Tour de Suisse victory.
